prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `b1fc8b8e`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 task package for `b1fc8b8e`. The rule is: the `6x6` input is a connected arrangement of four identical `2x2` motifs, either full `2x2` squares or the fixed three-cell L-shape `[[0,8],[8,8]]`; the output redraws that same motif in the four corners of a `5x5` grid with the center row and column left black. The verifier is in [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1fc8b8e/verifier.py#L4), the generator in [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1fc8b8e/generator.py#L25), and the package wiring in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1fc8b8e/__init__.py#L1).

I discarded the Sonnet hint and only used the Opus hint partially. The correction is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_b1fc8b8e/notes.md#L1): Opus had the right latent idea, but its non-square cell count was wrong (`12`, not `15`). The generator matches the family by sampling from the full connected `6x6` layout space of four non-overlapping identical motifs, then rendering the canonical corner layout for output. No new primitive was added.

Validation passed end to end: `7/7` official examples, `1000/1000` generated examples, and the shared builder produced [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b1fc8b8e/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b1fc8b8e/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b1fc8b8e/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b1fc8b8e/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/b1fc8b8e/previews/preview_03.png). I also opened a generated preview and it looks consistent with the official family.
